pi_items.source: setup_task.sh and export_result.sh explicitly establish this path for verification.

Code analysis of the synthetic DICOM generator shows a single slice yields ~901 bright vessel pixels. Simulating a 15-slice MIP projection gives ~3185 bright pixels, confirming 1500 as an effective lower threshold for evaluating a successful thick-slab MIP.

Code analysis of the synthetic DICOM generator reveals that a single thin slice produces ~901 bright pixels for the diagonal vessel (plus a minor amount for UI text annotations in the export), logically aligning with the 1200 upper bound for a thin slice.

privileged_info_summary: The task operates on a fully synthetic multi-slice CT dataset created at runtime by a python script in setup_task.sh. The agent must export the final MIP projection to exactly /home/ga/DICOM/exports/mip_projection.png. Verification confirms that the generated synthetic vessel yields ~901 bright pixels per 1mm slice (fitting the claimed 1200 maximum for a thin slice) while a 15mm thick-slab MIP accumulates ~3185 bright pixels, easily surpassing the min_bright_pixels_mip threshold of 1500.
